text: The learner docks the robotic system and inserts instruments, ensuring all are visible in the field of view. The learner then performs the ring tower transfer task, picking up a ring from the middle tower with one robotic arm, transferring it to the other arm, and placing it on the side tower without touching the wire, demonstrating end-to-wrist manipulation and hand-eye coordination.

The learner performs a surgeon's knot, ensuring the eyelets touch, followed by one throw. Next, horizontal mattress suturing is demonstrated through target points using an ovicle, with a button affixed at the beginning of the suture.

The learner uses one robotic arm to pick up a simulated vein and another arm for retraction, then switches to the monopolar scissors to cut the vein at marked locations. In the clover leaf dissection task, the learner carefully cuts a pattern on the latex skin layer without damaging the colored felt material underneath, demonstrating precise cutting and tissue handling skills.

The learner retracts the cut-out flap using the fourth robotic arm, exposing the underlying tissue. The vessel is dissected and cauterized with a bipolar instrument, followed by cutting with monopolar scissors. The exercise demonstrates manipulation, cutting, and dissection at various angles, utilizing multiple robotic arms for exposure and retraction.
